scope.purpose: Pin every exact Save330 Global Data payload to the authoritative xEdit FNV save layout, identify only branches that can be parsed fail-closed, and order the remaining parser and runtime work without inventing semantics.
scope.creditRule: This is an offline matrix. Only Global Data type 8 is currently engine-decoded. A layout observation, raw-field preservation, or proposed parser branch does not reduce semantic opacity until compiled engine code and exact/malformed fixtures pass.

layoutAuthority.authorityLimit: The source is authoritative for serialized field widths, order, arrays, and named branches. Names beginning Unknown/Unk and source comments do not establish runtime meaning; this matrix does not promote them into invented behavior.

encodingContract.entryEnvelope: little-endian u32 type, little-endian u32 payload length, then exactly payload-length bytes
encodingContract.terminatedScalars: Every xEdit *T scalar in these payloads has its fixed-width bytes followed by exactly 0x7C.
encodingContract.fixedWidthRule: Never split payloads on 0x7C. Raw values can contain 0x7C; type 3 RefID 0x00017C is followed by a second 0x7C terminator in the exact fixture.

referenceIds.guard: Decode the top two kind bits before the 22-bit value; for the exact one-based-index branch require 1 <= index <= 21755 before reading the little-endian u32 FormID table. Null is all zero. Unsupported kinds fail closed.

vsvalCounts.description: The low two bits select a 1-, 2-, or 4-byte encoded unsigned count; the count is the little-endian encoded integer shifted right two bits.
vsvalCounts.guard: Reject tag 3, non-canonical width, arithmetic overflow, count above parser limits, missing 0x7C, or a count whose fully expanded branch does not end exactly at the payload boundary.

encodingContract.lengthPrefixedStrings: For the exact wbLenStringT(...,-3) branch, read a terminated little-endian u16 length. When nonzero, read exactly that many bytes and a second 0x7C; length zero has no string-body field.
encodingContract.floatGuard: Reject non-finite floats. Type 5 contains the finite -FLT_MAX sentinel and tests must not reject it as infinity.
encodingContract.endGuard: Every supported type branch must consume its payload exactly. No unclassified tail can be silently accepted.

entry.failClosedGuard: Accept type 1000 only with a zero payload and exact envelope consumption; reject a nonzero payload. xEdit declares wbNull but its current decider also clamps results greater than 12 back to the unknown branch, which is harmless only because this exact payload is empty.

entries.definition: Global Data 0: u32-counted array of terminated u32 values
entries.failClosedDecode: Read one terminated u32 count, cap it before multiplication, require exactly count terminated u32 values, and require payload-end equality. Preserve the 43 values without assigning semantics.
entries.runtimeBlocker: xEdit establishes the array shape but not the meaning or consumer of each index; publication must wait for a retail/runtime semantic mapping.

entries.definition: Global Data 1: world/current-objective metadata with coordinates and level
entries.failClosedDecode: Parse the fixed layout and every terminator, require all three coordinate floats finite, resolve the three non-null RefIDs through the exact master/FormID table, and require payload-end equality.
entries.runtimeDomain: player world metadata, current quest objective, and level
entries.runtimeBlocker: This entry's (-18,0) pair is not the authoritative player exterior CELL. The Player ACHR position derives grid (-18,-1); type 1 must not overwrite that placement. The current-objective quest also requires the exact load-order store and quest-state publisher.

entries.definition: Global Data 2: TESActorBase list, square-size scalar, and size-by-size RefID matrix
entries.failClosedDecode: Decode the VSVal list count, each fixed RefID/u16 pair, then the terminated u32 matrix size. Check size*size overflow and caps before consuming exactly that many RefIDs. Accept nulls in this exact branch and require payload-end equality.
entries.runtimeBlocker: The matrix's gameplay meaning is not named, both exact actor-base keys are null, and no runtime consumer is mapped. Do not fabricate actor ownership, faction, or AI behavior from it.

entries.definition: Global Variables: VSVal-counted pairs of RefID and float
entries.failClosedDecode: Decode canonical VSVal 200, then exactly 200 fixed-width RefID/float pairs. Resolve every non-null RefID, reject non-finite floats and duplicate variable FormIDs, and require the record sequence to consume the payload exactly. Parsing by delimiter splitting is forbidden.
entries.runtimeDomain: global-variable values used by time, quests, dialogue conditions, factions, and world scripts
entries.runtimeBlocker: The native GLOB store and all condition/script readers must observe a single atomic saved-value overlay after exact load-order resolution; parsing alone cannot publish globals piecemeal.

entries.definition: Global Data 4: four terminated u32 fields and five fixed manager buckets containing nested lists
entries.failClosedDecode: Parse the four version-27 u32 fields and exactly five VSVal inner-list counts. The exact implementation may support only all-zero inner counts initially and must reject nonzero counts until the full nested bound-object/FormID/faction schema is implemented and tested.
entries.runtimeDomain: unknown manager buckets whose nonempty branch references bound objects, forms, and factions
entries.runtimeBlocker: All five Save330 buckets are empty, so the header can be preserved but no nonempty runtime behavior is evidenced or safely inferable.

entries.definition: Global Data 5: combat/pathing manager lists followed by five scalar floats
entries.failClosedDecode: Parse the u32 and three canonical VSVal counts. For the exact first slice require all three counts zero, then read five finite floats and exact payload end. Preserve -FLT_MAX as a finite sentinel. Reject any nonzero nested list until every pathing/count/conditional branch is implemented.
entries.runtimeDomain: combat groups, combatants, pathing locations, and AI-manager timing/scalars
entries.runtimeBlocker: Save330 proves only the empty-list branch. The nested schema is large and branch-dependent, and publication requires matching actor identities, navmesh/cell resolution, and AI/combat scheduling.

entries.definition: Global Data 6: fixed header plus declared-length raw bytes
entries.failClosedDecode: Parse the fixed fields and declared u32 length with overflow/cap checks, read exactly six raw bytes, require one trailing 0x7C and payload end. Keep the six-byte body semantically opaque; do not split it or interpret an array-of-arrays.
entries.runtimeBlocker: The authoritative source explicitly says the deeper array interpretation is truncated and hard to code. Raw preservation is structural evidence only and cannot earn semantic/runtime credit.

entries.failClosedDecode: Decode one canonical VSVal count and its terminator. The exact zero branch must end the payload; reject nonzero until both form-version floats and the image-space-modifier RefID branch are supported.
entries.runtimeDomain: active image-space modifiers and post-process state
entries.runtimeBlocker: The exact list is empty, which can prove absence only after normal load; it does not authorize any fallback post-process or shader override.

entries.definition: Sky: four weather RefIDs, time/transition state, flags, packed unknowns, fog power, and sky mode
entries.failClosedDecode: Existing engine coverage reads the exact 15 terminated fields, resolves current/default weather, rejects unsupported transition/override state, bounds time and blend values, and consumes the payload exactly. Preserve this as the regression baseline for every later Global Data slice.
entries.runtimeDomain: world time, sky, weather transition, fog, and renderer first-frame state
entries.runtimeBlocker: Native model resolution exists; atomic normal-save publication into the flat runtime and an admissible full-color Save330 frame remain pending.

entries.definition: Global Data 9: one u32 and a VSVal-counted nested spatial/reference list
entries.failClosedDecode: Read the terminated u32 and one canonical VSVal count. For Save330 require zero and exact payload end; reject nonzero until coordinate, RefID, nested-list, version-8 byte, and float validation are complete.
entries.runtimeDomain: unnamed spatial/transient manager list with coordinates and references
entries.runtimeBlocker: The exact branch is empty and the source does not name its runtime consumer; do not map it to actors, projectiles, effects, or placement without retail evidence.

entries.definition: Global Data 10: nested dialogue response state, voice filename, sound/topic/quest/speaker references, and radio list
entries.failClosedDecode: Follow every canonical count and HasDialogueItems branch, use width/length parsing rather than delimiter splitting, cap nested products and string bytes, validate all RefIDs before publication, require the exact one-item/one-response/18-radio tree to end at byte 494718, and reject unsupported branches atomically.
entries.runtimeDomain: radio selection, dialogue response state, voice/audio asset routing, topic/quest/speaker context
entries.runtimeBlocker: A parser must bind the exact INFO/TOPIC/QUST/speaker/SOUN references and voice asset to the live dialogue/radio/audio systems. Raw strings or resolved FormIDs alone do not make radio playback, subtitle state, result scripts, or saved dialogue position work.

entries.definition: Global Data 11: one terminated Sound RefID
entries.failClosedDecode: Read one fixed-width RefID and terminator, resolve it in bounds, validate that the resolved master record is a sound record before publication, and require exact payload end.
entries.runtimeBlocker: The native sound record, FNV-only archive asset, playback ownership, and save-resume semantics must all be connected; no Morrowind sound fallback is admissible.

recommendedParserAndTestOrder.reason: Keep the already-decoded 71-byte Sky state and zero-length type 1000 as permanent regression gates.
tests: weather RefID bounds and WTHR validation

recommendedParserAndTestOrder.reason: The regular 200-record layout removes 1,803 opaque bytes first and publishes quest/time-critical globals only after atomic validation.
tests: exact tail values and payload hash

recommendedParserAndTestOrder.reason: Fixed 57-byte player/objective metadata is small and gameplay-critical, but must retain the explicit non-authoritative-cell guard.
The four-byte Sound reference is the smallest meaningful FNV-only audio slice.
Decode the exact populated radio/dialogue/voice tree after reusable RefID/count/string guards exist.
tests: atomic rejection with no partial audio/dialogue publication

recommendedParserAndTestOrder.reason: Land the bounded integer vector and the regular actor-base square matrix while preserving their unknown semantics.
Support only Save330's empty nested manager branches plus fixed scalars; reject nonempty branches until full AI/pathing schemas are ready.
Prove the exact absence of image-space and unnamed spatial-list entries without installing fallback shader or world behavior.
Parse and preserve the declared-length envelope last; its six raw bytes remain semantically opaque until an independent runtime meaning is proven.

globalAcceptanceRules: Hash-lock the exact Save330 and xEdit definition source in every exact fixture.
Test successful exact parsing and targeted corruption at every count, length, terminator, RefID, float, branch discriminator, and payload boundary.
Resolve references against the exact saved master/load order and validate expected native record types before any state mutation.
Build a detached Global Data plan first; publish it atomically only after Player, changed forms, global tables, FormID tables, and all cross-record validation succeed.
Never convert a named Unknown/Unk field into gameplay behavior without retail/runtime evidence.
Keep semantic coverage at 71 decoded and 2,582 opaque until the corresponding engine parser slice compiles and exact plus malformed tests pass.
